conversation.text: I want to keep our team informed about new GitHub issues.
Sure — a few things to settle before I build. Which repo, where should the team see the notifications, and what events should trigger it?
Repo is n8n-io/n8n. Notifications go to our Slack channel #engineering. Only trigger on new issues — not on comments or labels or anything else.
Got it. Should the Slack message include the issue title, a link, and the author?
Yes, all three. Keep it concise.
Here's the plan: GitHub webhook listening for issues.opened events, then a Slack post to #engineering with title, link, and author. Want me to build it?
Built. The workflow is live and ready to receive GitHub webhook events at /github-new-issue.
One more thing — when an issue has the 'critical' label, also tag @oncall in the Slack message so the on-call rotation sees it. For non-critical issues, no tag.
Adding a conditional that checks for the 'critical' label and prepends @oncall to the message when present. Building now.

executionScenarios.name: non-critical-issue
executionScenarios.description: A regular new issue triggers a Slack notification without the @oncall mention
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The GitHub webhook receives a POST representing a new issue: { action: 'opened', issue: { number: 1234, title: 'Add export to CSV', html_url: 'https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/1234', user: { login: 'alice' }, labels: [] } }. The Slack node returns a success response with channel id and message ts.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A webhook trigger is configured for GitHub new-issue events. A Slack post node sends a message to the #engineering channel that contains the issue title ('Add export to CSV'), the issue URL, and the author login ('alice'). The message does NOT contain '@oncall' — the issue had no 'critical' label.

executionScenarios.name: critical-issue
executionScenarios.description: A new issue labelled 'critical' triggers a Slack notification that tags @oncall
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The GitHub webhook receives a POST representing a new critical issue: { action: 'opened', issue: { number: 1235, title: 'Production DB connection pool exhausted', html_url: 'https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/1235', user: { login: 'bob' }, labels: [{ name: 'critical' }, { name: 'production' }] } }. The Slack node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A Slack post is sent to the #engineering channel. The message contains the issue title, URL, and author. It also contains '@oncall' — the conditional correctly detected the 'critical' label and added the on-call mention. The Slack channel is #engineering (not any other channel).
